item.title: Crochet Class: Book Cover Granny Squares
item.description:
Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 3 PM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

While this class is beginner-friendly\, knowing the basics like a slip knot\, single crochet\, and double crochet will be necessary for this project. If you would like to join and don’t have these skills yet check out my beginner crochet class at Swoon City or find some good tutorials on YouTube. I taught myself to crochet before the Internet existed and have all the faith you can learn too! \nWe will make a classic granny square in this class\, but there are many variations of granny squares. Granny squares can create bags\, blankets\, sweaters\, and more! We will focus on making granny squares that resemble book covers. You can use the cover colors or even replicate the parts of the cover design! \nThis class will include all the colors necessary to create your book-themed granny square\, a guide to making granny squares\, and a hook. Please provide a picture of the cover you are looking to create to info@swoon.city after booking

From My Ballard

item.title: 2026 Seattle Sister Cities Soup Sampling
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: National Nordic Museum\, 2655 NW Market Street\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

Celebrate International Women’s Day at the 2nd annual Seattle Sister Cities’ Soup Sampling\, sponsored by the Seattle Sister Cities Association in partnership with the National Nordic Museum. The Soup Sampling is a community event where volunteer cooks from 11 Seattle Sister Cities share popular international soups representing four continents. Guests need to bring only a smile and an interest in tasting new recipes and learning about diverse cultures. Please register for the event before March 1st; walk-in admission will not be available. \nThis is a cultural event that includes five soup tastings per entry. Donations of $10 per person are encouraged and benefit the nonprofit Seattle Sister Cities Association to support cultural\, educational\, and humanitarian programs. (When reserving tickets\, your donation will result in 1 admission ticket. Use the quantity selector to reserve tickets for additional attendees). From Seattle’s 20 sister cities\, the following will be represented at this event: Reykjavik\, Iceland; Bergen\, Norway; Pecs\, Hungary; Be’er Sheva\, Israel; Christchurch\, New Zealand; Mombasa\, Kenya; Sihanoukville\, Cambodia; Perugia\, Italy; Surabaya\, Indonesia; Hai Phong\, Vietnam; and Kobe\, Japan. \nAttendees are also encouraged to visit New Nordic: Cuisine\, Aesthetics and Place as well as other exhibits at the National Nordic Museum and will get 50% off admission on the day of this event

From My Ballard

item.title: The Lowest Pair- Album Release Show w/ The Hackles
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Tractor, 5213 Ballard Avenue NW, Seattle, WA

The Lowest Pair has questions. The duo, made up of Kendl Winter and Palmer T. Lee, know that we tend to see duality as a problem. We want life to be linear, working through the dark to finally get to the light. Grief to joy, despair to hope, confusion to claritynot a jangly cycle we cant escape. But through their incandescent folk songs, the Lowest Pair often ask: What if we sit with the mess? What if thats not just more peaceful, but more magical, too? On their 8th album Always As Young As Well Ever Be, the Lowest Pair prove that over the last dozen years together, theyve become some of modern roots musics most mesmerizing, thoughtful purveyors. Produced by Tucker Martine (The Decemberists), the 10-track album puts the duos stark lyricism, string-driven arrangements, and raw compatibility on brilliant display and as a result, Always As Young As Well Ever Be pulses with life. The Lowest Pairs musicianship is another beautiful testament not just to playing that breathes, but playing that listens. Winter, who grew up in Arkansas but has lived in Washington State for the last two decades, and Lee, a Minnesota native, first gained attention as poetic singer-songwriters on banjos. While their family of strings has expanded, their fundamental approach hasnt: Respond to sounds and stories the other is making. This event is 21 and over. Any ticket holder unable to present valid identification indicating that they are at least 21 years of age will not be admitted to this event, and will not be eligible for a refund. This ticket is for admission to a live music venue. It provides the holder to observe a musical performance and nothing else. Other goods and services may be purchased once inside the venue. Please note, seating is limited and is available on a first come, first served basis. Price: $25.08 - $25.08

item.title: The Crocodile Presents: Bad Bad Hats w/Margaux
item.description:
Monday, March 9, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $24 ($32.45 after fees) Day of: $28 ($36.57 after fees) Bad Bad Hats: Bad Bad Hats is an indie rock band from Minneapolis, Minnesota. Kerry Alexander and Chris Hoge met in college and formed the band in 2012. In the 10 years of BBH, they have toured the country many times in their trusty minivan, sampling the best local cuisine along the way. BBH has toured with The Beths, Margaret Glaspy, The Front Bottoms, Hippo Campus, and Michelle Branch, among many others. Their fourth album Bad Bad Hats will be released April 12, 2024 on Don Giovanni Records.

item.title: Kids Embroidery Class: Fruit
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

Try embroidery with this beginner workshop. Kids (10+) will learn how to embroider fruits using 3 beginner embroidery stitches. This class is great for kids looking to get started with hand embroidery. They’ll learn how to place their fabric in the hoop\, transfer the pattern\, split apart their thread\, and stitch the satin stitch\, back stitch\, and sead stitch. Each attendee will receive a full kit that includes: a wooden embroidery hoop\, fruit transfer designs\, an embroidery needle\, full skeins of embroidery thread\, cream cotton fabric\, and printed instructions.

From My Ballard

item.title: The Swapanistas Present: Swap! A Women's Clothing Exchange
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Phinney Neighborhood Center\, 6532 Phinney Ave N Room 7\, Seattle\, 98103\, United States

Are you ready to swap up your style? Then join us for Swap 2026! Swap is an amazing event where you let go of your gently used clothing and accessories and trade them in for items that make you feel brand new\, while having fun and supporting a good cause: proceeds and clothing donations benefit local organizations for underserved women and kids. We are excited to announce that this year’s event is being hosted in partnership with the Phinney Neighborhood Association\, truly making this a community event! ​​Come check it out\, and bring your friends! Swap: Saturday March 14th\, 10 AM – 1:30 PM Item Drop-off: Friday March 13th\, 5 PM – 8 PM For tickets and more info\, please visit our website: https://www.swapanistas.org/ Please email us with questions: swapanistas@gmail.com Hope to see you there!

From My Ballard

Event details

item.title: Art Class: Nature Journaling & basics of making botanical Inks
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

Pause and see the natural world more closely through nature journaling and botanical inks. In this gentle\, hands-on class\, you’ll learn the basics of making ink from flowers and use these plant-based pigments to start a nature journal. You’ll also learn how considerations like paper and additives can give you different and exciting colors. All materials are included and each participant will take home a small sketchbook that we make in class and a 15ml pot of handmade floral ink—an invitation to notice new growth\, returning light\, and the unfolding of spring.

From My Ballard

item.title: Oscars Watch Party
item.description:
Sunday, March 15, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Urban Family Brewing\, 1103 NW 52nd St\, Seattle\, WA 98107\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

Come celebrate the 2026 Academy Awards! Urban Family Brewing and Film & Froth present the 2026 Oscars Watch Party hosted at the Urban Family Taproom in Ballard. Come grab a seat and watch the show while enjoying some wonderful Seattle beer and great company! Prizes to be raffled off include Urban Family Brewing gift cards\, merch\, and Film & Froth merch. Don’t forget to enter the Film & Froth Oscars Contest (https://www.filmfroth.com/oscars) to compete for more prizes! Kids and well behaved dogs on leashes are welcome downstairs in the taproom. The loft is 21+ and no animals are allowed. Check out Urban Family’s website for more information on their taproom! We hope to see you there to celebrate another amazing year of film with fantastic beer and the great people of Urban Family Brewing!

item.title: Kids On The Brink: Raising Emotionally Healthy Youth
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Zoom

Kids On The Brink: Raising Emotionally Healthy Youth In this event\, Donna Jackson Nakazawa will combine cutting-edge science\, real student voices\, and practical tools to help adults better support today’s youth. Donna will cover: – The latest research on female brain development – The unprecedented challenges facing teens today and why so many are in crisis – How these factors impact youth mental health – Why social stress\, in particular\, is the most damaging type of stress for kids – How regulating ourselves helps our kids regulate their emotions Participants will gain: – Actionable strategies parents\, families\, and organizations can utilize to better support our youth – 12 science-based steps for talking with youth in neuroprotective ways and building healthier parent-child connection Donna will also share highlights from her national Post-It Note Project\, featuring insights from thousands of middle and high school students about what they wish their parents and other adults understood—and did—differently. This event will be moderated by Christy Keating from The Heartful Parent Collective.

From My Ballard

Event details

item.title: Book Release Party: A Change of Pace
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

Join Swoon City & Generous Press to celebrate the launch of “A Change of Pace” by J.A. Stevens. 📚 Copy of A Change of Pace with signed bookplate Included in the ticket price Event will feature: 📖 A reading by Generous Press Co-founders 🍓Light bites and refreshments 🎭 Craft your own mask 📸 Photobooth 👯 Have a meet-cute with other bookish folks 👗 Regency dress is encouraged Description of A Change of Pace: This sapphic historical romance brings “pride without prejudice” to Regency London. Although cavalier with love\, the famously rakeish Miss Georgina Pace is deeply concerned for the well being of others. And so when she learns that dear innocent Mr. Coombes has been targeted by a corrupt gaming house\, she will stop at nothing to protect him. Miss Pace’s quest for justice leads her to the enigmatic Lady Mortimer\, who offers assistance while keeping her cards close to her chest. Will Miss Pace risk it all—including her heart? A Change of Pace reimagines a Regency London free of the entrenched systems of oppression we expect\, putting a speculative and hopeful lens onto the past.

From My Ballard

item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

Have you ever wanted to color with thread? In this class we’ll do just that! With hands-on guidance from fiber artist and author\, Melissa Galbraith\, you’ll enjoy a 3-hour workshop learning the basics of thread painting\, stitching on tulle fabric\, and stumpwork embroidery to create a pair of botanical earrings. No matter if you’re an experienced embroiderer or new to stitching\, in this class we’ll practice stitches needed to thread paint and create 3D ginkgo earrings. All materials are included for class to create wearable 3D embroidery.

item.title: Son Little CityFolk Tour 2026 w/ Tonina
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 8:30 PM

Location: Tractor, 5213 Ballard Avenue NW, Seattle, WA

Son Littles live show brings his music into a different kind of clarity- raw, expressive, and always evolving. Whether solo or with a full band, he moves fluidly between deep grooves, stripped-down intimacy, and moments that feel completely improvised yet emotionally precise. Its a performance style shaped by instinct and connection, grounded in the richness of his voice and the depth of his songwriting.Over the past decade, hes cultivated a sound that draws from soul, blues, folk, hip-hop, and Rs not just a setlist - its an experience.Following a run through the UK and Europe supporting Larkin Poe, Son Little is back with a new wave of music and a fresh stretch of tour dates for winter and spring. His new album CITYFOLK is set for release in March 2026, with recent singles in orbit and be better offering a first look at whats to come - both onstage and beyond in the months ahead. This event is 21 and over. item.title: Spring Dance
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: ARC Dance Space\, 9250 14th Avenue NW\, Seattle\, 98117\, United States

ARC Dance presents its annual Spring Dance performances featuring early dances from the 17th and 18th century re-created by Dance Historian\, Anna Mansbridge. The program also includes excerpts from the classic “La fille mal gardée”\, a charming comedic ballet choreographed by Artistic Director Marie Chong. The complete ballet will premiere on May 30\, 2026 at Shorewood Performing Arts Center. \nThis family-friendly performance takes the audience on a beautiful progression through dance history\, from early court dances to one of the oldest story ballets still being performed today. \nMarch 21 and 22 at 4pm\nARC Performance Space in Ballard/Crown Hill\, 9250 14th Ave NW\nTickets available at arcdance.org

From My Ballard

Event details

item.title: Impossible Loves Romance Improv Show at Swoon City
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

Welcome to a Impossible Loves\, a romance made up on the spot from your suggestions. Laugh\, care\, and swoon with your friend or foe as Impossible Loves improvises a brand new romance just for you! \nDiscover Impossible Loves\, a team of improvisers creating new worlds and new romances from your suggestions. Each one-hour improvised play opens by asking the audience about times they fell in love. From those moments\, Impossible Loves will create a brand-new romance show. Enjoy a world where anyone and anything can find a happy ending. \nThis show will take place at Swoon City.

item.title: Ballard Bookworms Community Storytime
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Location: Greenwood Public Library\, 8016 Greenwood Ave N\,\, Seattle\, 98103\, United States

Calling all bookworms! Ballard Preschool Co-op (BPC) is thrilled to present its FREE monthly Ballard Bookworms story time. Join BPC’s beloved Teacher Christine for a fun and engaging story time designed to spark a love of reading and build connections within our community. \nThis month our Special Guest is Emily Weissman (known online as Urb.Em.Sketches) a local urban sketcher\, author and illustrator. Emily’s watercolor paintings celebrate beloved neighborhood places that make Seattle extraordinary. She will be reading her children’s book\, My Home is With You\, a whimsical exploration of how home is not just a place\, but found in the hearts of those we love. \nBallard Bookworms is free and open to the public\, so be sure to invite your friends & neighbors! Recommended for ages 5 and under (older siblings welcome) \nThis program is organized by the Ballard Preschool Co-op and is held in a library branch meeting room. The Seattle Public Library does not sponsor or endorse this event.

From My Ballard

item.title: Finding Lucinda – Matinee Doc Screening & Set of Lucinda Williams Music
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 1:15 PM

Location: Tractor, 5213 Ballard Avenue NW, Seattle, WA

Music lovers and Lucinda Williams fans are invited to an inspiring Matinee event that blends live performance, documentary screening, and conversation as Finding Lucinda comes to The Tractor Tavern. This show celebrates the early life, creative spark, and lasting influence of the legendary singer-songwriter Lucinda Williams. To start the event, ISMAY and special guests will be performing Lucinda Williams songs.Finding Lucinda follows singer-songwriter ISMAY (Avery Hellman) on a transformative road trip through the American South, exploring the early life and enduring legacy of three-time GRAMMY-winning artist Lucinda Williams. Along the way, the film features never-before-seen footage, rare recordings, and interviews with Charlie Sexton, Buddy Miller, Mary Gauthier, and Lucinda herself.www.findinglucinda.com This event is 21 and over. item.title: Liz Cooper w/ Wes Parker
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $20 ($26.27 after fees) Day of: $25 ($31.42 after fees) Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry. Liz Cooper: The thing about finding yourself is there's always another corner to turn. The Vermont-based singer/songwriter Liz Cooper made her third album during a period of intense self-discovery and reinvention. She moved to New York for the first time, weathered a pandemic, came out to herself after falling in love with a friend, and experienced her first queer relationship and breakup, all in the course of a few years. New Day marks both a personal and a musical revolution for Cooper – a plunge into psychedelic pop depths and a fullhearted reflection of a whirlwind chapter in her life. These songs scintillate with the kind of self-confidence that only beams through after you've aimed a sharp gaze inward and realized that whatever you see will always keep darting ahead of you.

From Sunset Tavern

item.title: Print Class: Block Printing on Clothing
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

Have a stain on your favorite shirt that you need to cover up? Wanting to add a little extra flair to your favorite pair of jeans? Come learn block printing basics and print onto your clothes with Alessia! In this class you will learn the basics of block printing: -creating a design -carving your block -inking and getting a clean print Please bring your own garment(s) to print on and come prepared with an idea of what you’d like to print! The blocks we’ll be using are 4”x 6” – feel free to bring in a drawing or print-out and I’ll help you transfer it into your block. Or come with just an idea and I’ll help you translate it into something printable! Use our communal supplies in class\, or snag a block printing kit for 20% off if you buy it with your class ticket. Note: Textured fabrics like corduroy or knitted items are difficult to print on\, please bring something that’s nice and smooth (i.e. cotton t-shirt\, denim\, etc.)

From My Ballard

Event details

item.title: Intermediate Crochet Class: Mushroom Chapstick Holder
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

THIS IS AN INTERMEDIATE CLASS. BASIC CROCHET KNOWLEDGE REQUIRED. \nMake a crochet mushroom chapstick holder with Alessia! Attach it to your keychain and carry your chapstick with you wherever you go. Or use it as an ornament or bag charm! \nThis is an intermediate crochet class\, intended for folks who have done some crocheting before. These tiny mushrooms require us to work on a small scale\, which can be tricky for beginner crocheters. \nIf you want to learn how to crochet\, we recommend taking one of Anna’s classes first. \nWe will be using chains and single crochet to make these mini mushrooms\, so make sure you know those stitches. We’ll be using magic circles too\, but no worries if you haven’t done that before – we can teach you! All materials will be provided.

From My Ballard

item.title: The Crocodile Presents: The Thing, guests
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 9 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $20 ($28.33 after fees) Day of: $25 ($33.48 after fees) The Thing: What is The Thing? It’s a New York band, sure, but it’s also an ethos. A return to rock & roll’s roots — to garage bands toiling between the family minivans, to groups like the Beatles and their unique alchemy, to tape machines and live band recordings instead of slick studios and myriad songwriters. “We've kind of adapted the ethos of: with restriction comes creativity — old becomes new. And throughout every part of the process that remains true,” says guitarist/vocalist Jack Bradley. Never is that more apparent than on their self-titled third record, out August 6th on their own label. A rough and tumble suite of 12 songs that nods to everyone from The Kinks to The White Stripes, The Thing sounds like old New York revivicated and remixed. “It showcases all of us, all of our different personalities,” says bassist/vocalist Zane Acord. “In The Thing, we’re a collective band. We hang our hats on being a true band — where we all have the spotlight. I think that gives us a different edge.” That album follows 2023’s debut, Here’s the Thing, and The Things Is, which dropped the following year. The four members of Thing came from intersecting backgrounds, lending to their rock-and-roll-as-melting-pot vibe. Acord grew up with a drummer dad who hipped him to bands like Led Zeppelin and Grand Funk Railroad; he met guitarist/vocals Michael Carter — an avid Beatles fan — in middle school. The duo went on to collect Bradley in high school, an aspiring producer with a studio in his basement and a yen for psych rock. Jazz drummer Lucas Ebeling linked up with the band when everyone found their way to New York in 2022. They’ve since played more than 300 shows across the world. The band’s self-titled work, then, is a culmination of all those influences — and all that hustle. “We threw all of our different various influences throughout — all the decades of rock and roll and adjacent genres — and ended up with something of our own,” Bradley says. “Our contribution to the genre. Our style. Our… thing.”

From Sunset Tavern

item.title: Painting Class: Watercolor Landscapes for Beginners
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 2:30 PM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

This step-by-step\, instructor-led watercolor class is designed for beginners who want to learn how to paint beautiful landscapes with confidence. No prior experience is needed. You’ll be guided through the essential techniques used in watercolor landscape painting\, including: Basic and graded washes Composition fundamentals Layering to create depth and atmosphere Color mixing for natural\, harmonious tones With hands-on practice and individual guidance\, you will learn how to build a painting from start to finish and create your own completed landscape by the end of the session. The class is held in a relaxed and supportive environment\, making it a comfortable and encouraging space for total beginners. All materials and supplies are provided\, including a take-home watercolor kit (valued at $20) so you can continue practicing beyond the classroom. This class will take place at Swoon City.

item.title: Romance Writing Class
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

CLASSES WILL TAKE PLACE ON 4/7\, 4/14\, 4/21\, & 4/28 \nAre you a romance reader eager to learn how to write the genre you love? In this four-week class (eight hours of work total)\, you’ll learn the basics of plotting and writing a romance novel from scratch. No matter what romance subgenre calls your name\, you’ll learn to expand an initial idea\, center a romantic arc\, and build strong\, complementary characters. You’ll leave this collaborative\, workshop-based class with a plan for a novel (including character exercises\, a beat sheet\, and a synopsis) and at least one chapter of written prose. You’ll also get an overview of resources available to you once you finish your novel.

item.title: The Crocodile Presents: Westerman, Otto Benson
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 8:30 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Moved from Madame Lou's Advance: $29.50 ($38.11 after fees) Day of: $32 ($40.69 after fees) Westerman: Westerman is a London-born, Athens-based artist and songwriter signed to Partisan Records. Known for his thoughtful lyrics and genre-fluid sound, he has earned critical acclaim across two full-length albums and multiple EPs, with support from Pitchfork, Rolling Stone, The Guardian, Stereogum, NPR Music + more. After building early momentum with a series of singles and the Ark EP (2018), Westerman released his debut album Your Hero Is Not Dead in 2020. Produced with long-time collaborator Bullion (Nathan Jenkins), the album was recorded between Southern Portugal and London and received widespread critical praise. Pitchfork described the album as “excellent,” highlighting its fusion of soft rock, ambient textures, and lyrical introspection. Key tracks included “Blue Comanche,” “Think I’ll Stay,” and the title track, all of which received strong playlisting and airplay support across DSPs and BBC Radio. In 2023, Westerman returned with his sophomore LP An Inbuilt Fault, co-produced with Big Thief’s James Krivchenia. The album marked a sonic departure, incorporating more live instrumentation, polyrhythmic structures, and raw vocal delivery. It was developed during sessions in Los Angeles with a circle of experimental musicians and mixed by Krivchenia, expanding Westerman’s palette into more emotionally dynamic territory. The lead single “CSI: Petralona” was praised by Office Magazine and Stereogum, and accompanied by international touring, including sold out headline shows in NYC, LA, Chicago, and London. Across his career, Westerman has been featured in The New York Times, The FADER, and Clash, and has performed at key festivals including End of the Road and Primavera. His visual collaborations with renowned graphic designer Bráulio Amado have been widely recognized, forming a consistent visual identity across releases. Westerman will release another new album in 2025, more details on that to come!

From Sunset Tavern

Event details

item.description:

In this workshop with Angie Cooper of My Moon Macrame you will be taught the basic knotting techniques used in macrame. This class is great for beginners and intermediate levels. This project is designed to be altered to cater to students at any skill level. We provide craft supplies\, video tutorials and one-on-one guidance to help you create your macrame project. During this class you will learn to macrame a lanyard keychain. Your instructor\, Angie from My Moon Macrame will teach you the knots and provide step by step instructions to help you complete your project. We provide all craft supplies\, video tutorials and one-on-one guidance. This project is designed to cater to students at any skill level. You will go home with a completed macrame lanyard keychain and all the tools you need to continue your macrame journey at home. Supplies: 4mm Oeko Tex Recycled Cotton Cord Key Ring Scissors Tape

item.title: Glass Class: Book Cover Coasters
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

Cozy-up with a mug of tea and your favorite book… but oh no! Where are all the coasters? Have no fear! We’ve got just the solution to protect your precious objects from destructive heat and moisture — glass book coasters. That’s right! Coasters designed by you to look just like your favorite book cover or classic novels\, so that you can enjoy your next book with some extra special\, on-theme decor. \nIn this 3-hour glass charm workshop\, you will build unique compositions using Elmer’s glue and a variety of glass dots\, stringers\, shapes\, and more. Learn the fundamentals of fusing while experimenting with a new medium and exploring the many possibilities! Cost includes all tools and supplies as well as materials for up to 2 coasters per person. Finished pieces will be ready for pick-up at the workshop location after 1 week.

item.title: Jarv x RDGLDGRN w/ Damn Skippy
item.description:
Sunday, April 12, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $25 ($31.93 after fees) Day of: $30 ($37.08 after fees) Jarv: Brooklyn-based MC / Producer, Jarv started making music in 2009 and put out his first official release "Jarvage Vol.1" in 2016. He has since released four full-length projects in his modern yet classic boom-bap style. He has garnered millions of streams and YouTube views and has toured with the likes of RA The Rugged Man, Masta Ace, Little Stranger, Mr. Lif & Akrobatik, Sadat X, Spose, The Palmer Squares and so on. RDGLDGRN: RDGLDGRN have always been about the blending of worlds. Even their name stems from the merging of its member’s diverse influences and personalities, each represented by a corresponding color - Red (Guitar), Gold (Bass) and Green (Vocals). On LP3, they’ve managedto set a new bar for how contrasting sounds can come together. With that being said, it’s no surprise that it is also the record they’ve had the most control over. After learning the many lessons of navigating the major label system over the past six years, the band will be releasing LP3 on their own label, Deuce Day World. It is an album that the band feels finally captures the idea RDGLDGRN was based. “Everyone views us in a different way,” Green adds, “and in the past that has defined our music. On LP3, the music captures exactly how we see ourselves”. Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry.

item.title: Glenn's Last Ride Acoustic Tour: Logan Mize w/ Keelan Donovan
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Tractor, 5213 Ballard Avenue NW, Seattle, WA

Ten years ago before earning gold records, Logan toured in his 89 station wagon named Glenn and developed a devoted following of fans across the country that loved his unique and relatable experience of crowd-sourced touring. This spring as an homage to how Logan toured in his station wagon, weve rebooted the Glenn tour for one last ride. Over his nearly two-decade career, Logan has risen as a top independent artist, bending the country genre with his unique heartland rock sound and racking up over 500 million streams. Hes shared the stage with legendary acts including Eric Church, Jamey Johnson, Sawyer Brown, Wade Bowen and many more. Logans singles Better Off Gone and Grew Apart have both earned RIAA Gold certification. Logans next full-length record Cottonwood Falls will be released this spring. This event is 21 and over. item.title: Rocketship, The Telephone Numbers, Remedy & Wren
item.description:
Thursday, April 16, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $20 ($26.27 after fees) Day of: $25 ($31.42 after fees) Rocketship “A Certain Smile, A Certain Sadness” 30th Anniversary Re-issue and West Coast Tour Some of the best records are those that transcend the usual parameters of music to create worlds of their own. When it’s clear that what you’re listening to isn’t just a collection of that year’s songs, but a deeply thought-out, architected space where ideas clash, converse, interlock; these are the albums that end up lasting well beyond their era, changing with us, and revealing new surprises every time we return to them. One of the clearest examples of an album that crafts a strange and beautiful world not quite like any other is Rocketship’s 1996 full-length debut A Certain Smile, A Certain Sadness. Even upon arrival, this was an experience unto itself, and 30 years later, these eight songs of bittersweet bliss still feel new. Slumberland is delighted to release a 30th Anniversary edition of this instant classic, making it readily available on vinyl and CD once again after years of unavailability and collector demand. Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry.

item.title: Art Class: Handmade Water Marbled Paper
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 1:30 PM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

Step into the mesmerizing world of water marbling\, a centuries-old art technique where color floats\, swirls\, and dances across the surface of water before being transformed into stunning\, one-of-a-kind designs. In this immersive\, hands-on workshop\, you’ll learn how to create beautiful marbled patterns by manipulating paint on water and transferring your designs onto paper and other surfaces. You’ll experiment with color\, movement\, and tools to create unique marbled prints on paper\, fabric\, and small 3-D objects. This class is designed for beginners and curious creatives alike – no prior art experience is needed. All supplies are provided\, and step-by-step guidance will ensure everyone can successfully create multiple finished pieces to take home. Along the way\, we’ll talk about troubleshooting common challenges and how to continue marbling at home with accessible materials. Water marbling is both meditative and playful\, inviting experimentation and embracing the unexpected. Come ready to slow down\, get a little messy\, and enjoy the process of creating something truly unique. The class will cover the following: 1. The four foundational patterns (stone\, chevron\, getgel\, and nonpareil) 2. Tools\, materials\, and how to prepare for a water marbling session 3. Historical grounding into this art form 4. Water marbling examples from around the world 5. Tips and tricks for water marbling at home

From My Ballard

item.title: Summer Cannibals, Divers, guests
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 9 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $15 ($20.09 after fees) Day of: $18 ($26.27 after fees) Summer Cannibals: Summer Cannibals broke up. Three years later, they made a new album. More on that later. In the meantime, 2026 marks the 10-year anniversary of their acclaimed third record, Full Of It, released by NW punk mainstay Kill Rock Stars. To mark the anniversary, the band will play their first headlining shows since 2022, performing the album front to back in Portland at Mississippi Studios and Seattle at Sunset Tavern with longtime friends and Portland legends Divers. Full Of It, the album Pitchfork praised for “expertly balancing flame-belching Mad Max riffage with lyrics frankly exploring questions of codependence and need,” captured a turning point for the band, sharpening their raw, hook-driven sound into something more focused and intense. The anniversary is being marked with a vinyl repress, two Northwest shows, and a new album arriving later this year. Is this a victory lap? No, not really. It’s a reset, a return and whatever happens when you decide you aren’t actually finished. Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry.

item.title: Emily Nenni w/ guests
item.description:
Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Tractor, 5213 Ballard Avenue NW, Seattle, WA

Emily Nenni and Movin Shoes is all about the right fit. Made in Memphis, the Nashville based singer-songwriters fourth full length album is as much about dancing as following the steps of understanding and respect. Nennis songs reflect her solicitude, but are much more soulful in this setting - taking inspiration from the many impassioned performers of the region, to 60s girl groups and honky tonk heroes.Pairing once again with producer John James Tourville (The Deslondes), Movin Shoes was cut at Matt -Ross-Spangs new Southern Grooves studio. The 13 tracks tie up nicely with added covers from Paul Simon, Delbert McClinton and Cass Elliott. Movin Shoes will be released on May 1st via New West Records. This event is 21 and over. item.title: Embroidery Class: Sashiko Tea Towel Workshop
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

Learn the basics of Japanese sashiko hand stitching and apply it to embellish a tea towel. Sashiko is a Japanese running stitch traditionally used to mend and strengthen textiles and clothing\, as well as creating geometric patterns. In this hands-on 2 hour workshop\, learn the basics of Japanese sashiko stitching and apply it to embellish a tea towel. You will learn how to work with sashiko needles and thread and sew a running stitch. Each student will take home a set of sashiko needles\, 2 skeins of sashiko thread\, and a cotton tea towel. Sashiko is an excellent way to slow down and stitch with mindfulness. There is such beauty in its imperfection and its simplicity\, flexibility and adaptability to many projects! This workshop is for sewers of all levels\, from beginners to experienced. This class will take place at Swoon City.

From My Ballard

item.title: Jonny Fritz, guests
item.description:
Monday, April 27, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $20 ($26.27 after fees) Day of: $25 ($31.42 after fees) Jonny Fritz: Jonny Fritz – a veteran of 15 years as a touring musician. Born in Missoula, Montana and raised in Esmont, Virginia. A near perfect blend of country and western, yet impossible to know what to call it or do with it. Jonny calls it Dad Country; a theme park tailor made for dad’s. That’s his genre (so he says) and if that makes sense to you then you ought to have your head checked. The guy does it all. He is a famed leatherworker known for covering household items in hand- tooled leather (from microwaves and coffee grinders to telecasters). He’s made guitar straps for all your favorite bands and he even co-wrote the hit song All Your Favorite Bands (with Taylor Goldsmith of Dawes). Currently residing in the foothills of Los Angeles, he hasn’t toured much in the past few years since focusing on selling houses and raising his daughter. He’s branded himself as L.A.’s Only RealtorTM and has become quite successful at selling multi-million dollar properties to the stars. Not to mention he’s the Music Director of a boutique music venue in Sonoma County, CA called Little Saint that has garnered some major headlines for their surprise shows featuring the likes of boygenius, Phoebe Bridgers, Rufus Wainwright, Hiss Golden Messenger, Lavender Country and so many more. There’s a special freakiness Fritz brings to whatever he does. Selling real estate, running marathons, songwriting, or leather-working. It somehow all makes sense once you learn to make sense of it. Catch this delicate flower the next time you smell him coming! You won’t want to miss out on your chance to see him do his thing. Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry.

From Sunset Tavern

item.title: Happy Landing, Lostboycrow
item.description:
Tuesday, May 5, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $22 ($28.33 after fees) Day of: $25 ($31.42 after fees) Happy Landing: HAPPY LANDING is an American band formed in Oxford, Mississippi in 2020 by Matthew Hendley (lead vocals, guitar), Keegan Christensen (vocals, keys), Jacob Christensen (drums, vocals), Andrew Gardner (fiddle, vocals), and Wilson Moyer (bass/guitar, vocals). The group pioneers a new wave of folk music, captivating audiences internationally with their energetic live shows and fresh sound, blended from the roots of southern rock, punk and folk. After debut festival appearances at Bonnaroo, Lollapalooza, and Summerfest, the band’s sophomore album, Big Sun, comes in 2026 paired with a major headline tour of the US & Canada. Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry.

From Sunset Tavern

Event details

item.title: Sam Blasucci, guests
item.description:
Thursday, May 7, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

$15 ($20.09 after fees) Sam Blasucci: Physical Dream is Sam Blasucci’s fourth studio album and first double LP. It’s a sensorial, immersive record that shimmers, shudders, and grooves with genre-blurring mastery. The 28-track album unfolds like a living body—nonlinear and dynamic, alive with cascading instrumentals, buoyant boogie rhythms, and a sound that is simultaneously groovy and poetic. Recorded over just two weeks on a remote island property in Sooke, British Columbia, Physical Dream was born close to the ground. Blasucci slept in a small shack on the land while working closely with engineer and collaborator Jonny Payne. Additional recording took place in studios across Los Angeles and Ojai, California. The album includes contributions from Mia Wilson (vocals), Randal Fisher (horns, flute), Andres Renteria (percussion), Laena Myers (violin, viola, vocals), and Leng Bian (harp). Aside from traditional instrumentation, the album also gives voice to the environments where the music was made. Night sounds recorded on Blasucci’s Ojai property appear on “WHO LIVES ON THESE LANDS.” Running water from a Big Sur waterfall appears on “ORCHIDS.” “SI SE PUEDE” includes audio recorded at an anti-ICE protest in Oxnard, California. These elements expand the album’s sense of space and relationship. Blasucci’s voice is deeply personal and human, yet constantly in dialogue with a wider living world—and asking it to dance. Lyrically, Physical Dream holds romance, mysticism, and political resistance side by side. Musically, the album feels like Leonard Cohen, Prince, and Brian Eno gave birth to a bilingual, fantastical, hypnotic phantom dance baby. Animated by introspection and motion, Physical Dream invites listeners into reflection and movement in equal measure. Born in Northeast Los Angeles, Blasucci has lived across the U.S. and Mexico, including Coahuila, New Orleans, and Orem, and now finds home in Ojai, California. He learned music from his father as a child and formed Mapache at age 20, releasing 5 albums before launching his solo career in 2023. Physical Dream will be released this spring on vinyl and all streaming platforms. Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry.

From Sunset Tavern

item.description:
Thursday, May 14, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $22.50 ($30.90 after fees) Day of: $25 ($33.48 after fees) Coma Cinema: Coma Cinema is the longest running project of songwriter and producer, Mathew Lee Cothran, known for his work as Elvis Depressedly and The Goin’ Nowheres. Coma Cinema has been active since 2005, remaining independent and happily obscure in an ever dulling world. Coma Cinema is committed to fighting entropy by the alchemy of art, bringing thoughts to life in Cothran and his listener’s minds with a spirit of deep gratitude for those who have listened to and shared his music for many years.

From Sunset Tavern

item.title: Jonah Marks (Album Release: POEMS) w/Special Guests
item.description:
Friday, May 15, 2026 at 8:30 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $15 ($20.09 after fees) Day of: $20 ($26.27 after fees) Jonah Marks: Jonah Marks is an independent singer-songwriter and rapper crafting soulful, story-driven lyrics through Hip Hop and R&B. After spending more than a decade working behind the scenes in artist management and concert production, Jonah Marks steps fully into his own creative voice on POEMS, a 10-song narrative that challenges modern disconnection and overreliance on technology. The album opens with “Poems Over Cell Phones,” a thesis-driven track that sets the tone for a journey away from constant digital output and toward presence and creativity. As the record unfolds, its production becomes increasingly organic and acoustic, culminating in the community-focused closing track, “People Over Poems.” POEMS is primarily produced by Elan Wright, with additional production from Heavywait, Noah Coinflip, and Damono. The album features rich live instrumentation, including piano, strings, percussion, choir vocals, and a guest feature from hip-hop artist Rell Be Free. Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry.

From Sunset Tavern

item.title: Old Mervs, guests
item.description:
Saturday, May 23, 2026 at 8:30 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $25 ($31.68 after fees) Day of: $30 ($36.83 after fees) Old Mervs: An unbreakable bond shared between lifelong mates, Dave and Henry hail from the tiny farming community of Kojonup, a town three hours south of Perth, Western Australia. Starting out as school friends and rehearsing in a makeshift space in an old farm shed, Old Mervs are a far cry from their humble beginnings. Riding a wave of wins, Old Mervs are celebrating a massive 2025 after the success of their ARIA-charting debut album release and sold out tours across Australia, the United States, the UK and Europe. Recorded with ARIA award-winning producer Chris Collins, their debut self-titled album scored coveted Australian youth broadcaster triple j’s Feature Album of the week and landed at #1 on the ARIA album charts, winning hearts with hits “Parched“, “What You’ve Lost”, “Forget It”, “Everyone Will See It” and “Don’t Go”. In 2025, the pair embarked on a mammoth 20 date album tour across Australia and New Zealand along with their biggest headline home-state show to date at Perth HPC. They then headed to the US to play at world-renowned festival Lollapalooza alongside names like Olivia Rodrigo, Tyler the Creator and Sabrina Carpenter, bookended by a massive run of headline shows across the States, stopping in for a Audiotree session too. They closed out the year with a return to the UK and their first ever shows in Europe. This year looks to be just as big, if not bigger. With new music in the pipeline, and a stack of touring at home and across the globe, Old Mervs are thrilled to return to the US again for a stack of huge headline shows, along with Kilby Block Party, Gov Ball and more dates to be announced! Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry.

From Sunset Tavern

item.title: The Crocodile Presents: The Scratch, guests
item.description:
Sunday, May 24, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $23.50 ($31.93 after fees) Day of: $25 ($33.48 after fees) The Scratch: Formed in 2016 after their previous tech and groove-metal outfit Red Enemy had dissolved, The Scratch are a band that doesn’t seem to fit in anywhere, but have proven to fit in everywhere. Starting out with just acoustic guitars and a wooden box, the band created an immediate buzz in their hometown of Dublin, Ireland. Seemingly uncapable of keeping themselves boxed in to one singular genre, they meander intentionally around heavy metal, Irish folk music, stadium rock, and post punk. Constantly evolving both creatively and sonically, never resting on their laurels, never taking themselves too seriously, while always putting their souls into the music. They have won the hearts of anyone that has experienced their live shows, and have garnered a cultlike following in their native Ireland, the UK and most recently mainland Europe and North America.

From Sunset Tavern

item.title: Alela Diane, guests
item.description:
Thursday, June 18, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $20 ($26.27 after fees) Day of: $25 ($31.42 after fees) Alela Diane: More than a decade into one of contemporary folk’s most quietly extraordinary careers, Alela Diane returns on May 22 with Who's Keeping Time? via Fluff & Gravy / Loose Music. The Portland songwriter’s seventh full-length album came as the consequence of intuition, coincidence, and community. On the first of April last year, Michael Hurley, folk legend and indispensable presence in the Portland music scene, died at the age of eighty-three. “I was absolutely gutted,” says Alela, who didn’t just revere Hurley but knew him well. She performed in a tribute show for Hurley, and in that collective mourning, found solace and inspiration. “It was an epiphany to realize how much I missed my community. I felt very clear about what I wanted in that moment—I want to be alive. I want to see live music. I want to play it.” Though she’d taken the solo route on her last handful of releases, Alela was interested in a new way forward. Plucking the day away in the attic of her 1892 Victorian home, she found new songs flickering in the dusty light—and a desire to play them with people. Alela met drummer Danny Austin-Manning at Clay Street Studios one night and he introduced her to co-producer Sam Weber (Madison Cunningham, Anna Tivel). Along with Sebastian Owens on bass, they recorded fifteen songs in less than five days—all live in the very attic where she wrote them. Anna Tivel contributed backing vocals and violin. Peter Lalish, of the band Lucius, added guitar. Fellow Pacific Northwesterners Kati Claborn and Luke Ydstie of Blind Pilot and AC Sapphire provided overdubs of all kinds. Alela’s vision for uninhibited music and revived, creative kinship thrust her into a new season. The result is an 11-track set ranging from lulling to raw to cinematic, with Alela’s mellifluous voice a lively and affecting instrument throughout. The lyrics reveal an artist with the particular kind of strength required to face pain without getting lost—an ability Alela has honed over a lifetime of songwriting, during which her lustrous discography has garnered major critical acclaim from the likes of Pitchfork, NPR Music, The Guardian, and plenty more. UNCUT has characterized her skill as “insanely beautiful, with the strength and delicacy of spider silk” while counting her work among the ‘50 best singer-songwriter albums’ of all time—a canon comprising John Lennon, Joni Mitchell, Joan Baez, and Paul Simon. Consequence has echoed that significance, declaring, “Hers is a timeless sound, that of a wayfaring troubadour, which only seems to come a few times a generation.” With Who’s Keeping Time?, Alela Diane transforms grief into gathering, reaffirming her place as a vital, living force in contemporary folk music—rooted in community, presence, and the enduring power of song. Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry.

From Sunset Tavern

A melting pot of surf, psych, folk, and indie. Based out of the mountains of British Columbia, the group has released two full length albums since 2022 and performed relentlessly across western Canada. item.title: Mariel Buckley, guests
item.description:
Sunday, June 28, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $20 ($26.27 after fees) Day of: $25 ($31.42 after fees) Mariel Buckley: Unapologetically genuine, wry and soft-hearted, Mariel Buckley has planted her flag firmly as a songwriter who’s in it for the long haul. She aims to find herself squarely on the fringes and exactly where she belongs: singing and writing songs for the underdogs. Raised in the heart of Canada’s rodeo and country music scenes, she was destined to challenge the boundaries around her, both in life and in song. With a fearless spirit that’s consistently broken through both genre and personal barriers, Buckley’s unique blend of angst-driven alt-folk has made her a standout artist, offering a fresh and dynamic perspective on modern Americana music. Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry.

item.title: The Crocodile Presents: Soda Blonde, guests
item.description:
Sunday, September 27, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $22.50 ($30.90 after fees) Day of: $25 ($33.48 after fees) Soda Blonde: Soda Blonde are a four-piece alt-pop band from Dublin, Ireland, formed in 2019 by Faye O’Rourke (vocals), Adam O’Regan (guitar/keys), Donagh Seaver-O’Leary (bass) and Dylan Lynch (drums). The group emerged from the ashes of critically acclaimed indie rock outfit Little Green Cars, moving toward a bolder, more cinematic and emotionally charged sound that blends evocative lyrics with adventurous production. Their music traverses the tension between introspection and ambition, pairing O’Rourke’s distinctive, expressive vocals with genre-defying arrangements that draw comparisons to synth-infused art-pop and emotionally honest songwriting. Soda Blonde’s debut single “Swimming Through the Night” immediately marked a stylistic shift for the members, establishing the band’s commitment to cinematic hooks and atmospheric grooves. The band’s first studio album Small Talk (2021) showcased polished, self-produced songwriting and was nominated for the Choice Music Prize. Their sophomore record Dream Big (2023) expanded their sonic palette and solidified their reputation as one of Ireland’s most compelling contemporary acts. In 2025, Soda Blonde released the People Pleaser EP and completed an ambitious live orchestral project with the National Symphony Orchestra at Dublin’s National Concert Hall, captured in both a concert film and a live album. Known for their emotionally charged live performances, bold visuals and genre-spanning sound, Soda Blonde continue to push beyond conventional pop boundaries while maintaining a deeply personal connection to their lyrics and visual storytelling. Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry.

From Sunset Tavern
